items.headline: Lawsuit filed against city over SummerHill Homes development
items.summary: Mark Fantozzi has filed a lawsuit (Case No. 26CV492787) against the City of Cupertino and developer SummerHill Homes, LLC. The city council discussed the case in closed session on May 11, indicating active litigation over what appears to be a housing development dispute.

items.headline: City approves 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: Palo Alto approved a six-story, 100% affordable housing development with 72 units on a 0.43-acre parcel at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city-owned surface parking lot. The project qualifies for state density bonus concessions due to its affordable housing status.

items.headline: Major electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive research campus
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted at 3210 Porter Drive to support new R&D tenant improvements, including new transformers, switches, and conduit extending to a substation on Hanover Drive. The upgrade signals continued demand for research and development space in Palo Alto's commercial corridor.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: The city council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement activities. This policy limits cooperation with federal immigration authorities on municipal land and facilities.

items.headline: Hazardous vegetation declared public nuisance in Los Gatos
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance, enabling the city to order property owners to clear dangerous vegetation. This gives the city enforcement authority to address fire risk and safety hazards on private and public land.

items.headline: San José amends digital signage agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: The City Council approved changes to its contract with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This amendment affects advertising and public information displays across San José locations.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: San José released a Request for Proposals for encampment cleanup services, signaling a formal procurement process to manage unhoused encampment sites across the city.

items.headline: City negotiating land deal with school district at Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District over property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with City Manager Tina Kapoor and the Interim City Attorney handling talks on price and payment terms. The outcome could signal a significant real estate transaction or partnership between the city and schools.
